Additional context from HEARTBEAT.md:
# Heartbeat Checklist

> Keep this small to limit token burn. Rotate through checks, don't do all every time.

## When to Check (2–4x/day)

- Morning (08:00–10:00)
- Midday (12:00–14:00)
- Evening (18:00–20:00)
- Late check only if something might be urgent

## Things to Check (rotate, don't do all)

- [ ] **Email** — any urgent unread?
- [ ] **Calendar** — events in next 24–48h?
- [ ] **Docker** — any stopped containers that should be running?
- [ ] **Projects** — git status on active repos
- [ ] **Memory** — review recent `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, distill to `MEMORY.md`

## When to Reach Out

- Calendar event coming up (< 2h)
- Docker container unexpectedly down
- Important email arrived
- It's been > 8h since last message

## When to Stay Quiet (HEARTBEAT_OK)

- Late night (23:00–08:00) unless urgent
- Nothing new since last check
- Just checked < 30 min ago

Looking at the previous session's transcript, from around 12:39 UTC (7:39 AM CDT) through 15:50 UTC (10:50 AM CDT), there were multiple consecutive failures with the error `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND api.kimi.com` — meaning DNS couldn't resolve the Kimi API endpoint. content.text:
Found the issue. Here's what went wrong in the previous session:

**DNS / Network failure to Kimi API**
- **~7:39 AM → 10:50 AM CDT**: Multiple heartbeats (and any messages you may have sent) failed with `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND api.kimi.com` — your machine couldn't resolve the Kimi API domain at all. Likely a DNS or network hiccup.
- **~7:39 PM → 8:11 PM CDT**: More failures, this time generic `fetch failed` — the API was unreachable again.

So basically: I was alive, the gateway was running, but I couldn't reach Kimi to actually think/respond. Everything I tried to send failed before producing any content.

The session finally got reset at 21:39 CDT (when you messaged me just now), which is why we're in a fresh session.

Everything looks healthy now — the current session is responding normally. Was there something specific you were trying to ask during the outage, or did you just notice I was ghosting you?
